member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartPoint
member.summary: The single, generic data point every BlazorDX chart accepts — replacing the previous per-chart bespoke shapes (ChartBar, parallel X/Y lists, a bare Values list, and per-series ChartSeries arrays). Not every field applies to every chart: a bar/pie/funnel/sparkline chart reads + ; a line/area/scatter chart reads + ; a stacked-bar/radar chart also reads to group points onto a shared category/axis list; a candlestick chart reads .. as Open/High/Low/Close. A field a given chart type doesn't use is simply ignored. Plain record struct — no reflection, no per-consumer mapping step (a [ChartRow] source generator for projecting an existing domain type onto this shape is planned as a follow-up).
member.param: X-axis value (line/area/scatter charts).
The primary value: bar/pie/funnel/sparkline magnitude, an XY chart's Y, or a candlestick's Open.
Category / axis-label key: the bar/pie/funnel/sparkline label, the stacked-bar/radar category-or-axis key, or the candlestick's label.
Candlestick High.
Candlestick Low.
Candlestick Close.
Series name, for charts with more than one series (stacked bar, radar).
Optional CSS color override for this point/series; otherwise a palette color is used.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartLegendToggledEventArgs
member.summary: A legend entry's visibility was toggled on a multi-series chart (pie, stacked bar, radar). The chart already hides/shows the corresponding slice or series itself when this fires — the event is for a host that wants to react (e.g. update an external summary), not required plumbing.
member.param: The toggled entry's key: a pie slice's , or a stacked-bar/radar name.
The entry's new visibility state.

member.summary: Which field a -tagged property maps onto. // accept a property of any type (stringified via Convert.ToString, so an int or enum category works as-is); //// require a numeric-convertible property — a non-numeric property tagged with one of these is silently not mapped (the field keeps its default), the same non-numeric-columns-degrade-gracefully policy [GridColumn] already uses.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartRowAttribute
member.summary: Marks a domain type as projectable onto . The BlazorDX.SourceGen generator emits a ToChartPoints() extension for every type carrying this attribute (one per row, built from its -tagged properties), so charts bind existing domain data with zero runtime reflection — the same zero-reflection story [GridRow] already tells for the DataGrid.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartValueAttribute
member.summary: Declares a property as the source of one field. See .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.BulletPoint
member.summary: One KPI row for (Stephen Few's bullet-graph design): a single measure bar against a target marker and, optionally, qualitative range bands (e.g. poor/satisfactory/good). Doesn't fit the flat shape — a bullet row has its own scale () and multiple thresholds, not one X/Y pair.
member.param: The KPI's name.
The measure — how far the bar fills.
Where the target tick is drawn.
The scale's upper bound; and read against [0, Max].
Ascending thresholds within [0, Max] splitting the track into qualitative bands (e.g. [40, 75] for poor/satisfactory/good). null draws a single plain track.
Optional CSS color override for the measure bar; otherwise a palette color is used.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartGradients
member.summary: Shared SVG <defs> gradient rendering for charts that opt into a Gradient parameter. Each gradient fades a mark's own color from full opacity at its base to translucent at its tip — no hardcoded lighter-shade math, so it works with any color (hex, named, a CSS variable) and stays theme-safe. One <linearGradient> is emitted per distinct color actually in use.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.ChartGradients.Url(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: The fill value referencing the gradient built for .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartTreeNode
member.summary: A hierarchical data node for and . A leaf (no ) carries its own ; a parent's effective value is always the sum of its children's, computed by the chart — on a node that has children is ignored. Doesn't fit the flat shape (it's a tree, not a series). Plain record, no reflection.
member.param: The node's name.
The leaf's magnitude; ignored on a node with children.
Optional CSS color override; otherwise a palette color (by top-level sibling index) is used.
Child nodes, or null/empty for a leaf.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxAccordion
member.summary: Tier 2 styled accordion. Inherits all behavior from and renders a list of disclosure sections with WAI-ARIA semantics. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-layout.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxAlert
member.summary: An inline message banner with a severity (info / success / warning / error), optional title, and optional dismiss button. Styling is CSS-variable driven.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxAreaChart
member.summary: An area chart: a single series rendered as a filled SVG polygon with a stroked top edge. Like it offloads LTTB downsampling to the compute backend (Rust in the browser) so a huge series draws as a few hundred points. Styling is token-driven (see dx-chart.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxAreaChart.Points
member.summary: The series to plot, in order. Only is read — points are laid out evenly by index (matching the prior Values-only behavior), so is ignored.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxAreaChart.Threshold
member.summary: Approximate number of points to draw.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxAvatar

member.summary: Image source. When set, the image is shown instead of initials.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxAvatar.Initials

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarChart
member.summary: A categorical bar chart rendered as scaled SVG rectangles with value and category labels. Vertical columns by default; set for a horizontal bar chart. Styling is token-driven (see dx-chart.css).
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement: wiring and/or turns the chart into a keyboard-navigable widget (role="application", roving aria-activedescendant, arrow/Home/End, Enter/Space to select) — mirroring the DataGrid/Scheduler/Calendar active-cell pattern via . With neither wired, the chart stays exactly the pre-existing decorative role="img" with no behavior change.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarChart.Gradient
member.summary: Fill each bar with a top-to-bottom fade of its own color instead of a flat fill.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarChart.OnPointSelected
member.summary: Raised on click or Enter/Space when a bar is the active (keyboard-focused) point.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarChart.OnPointHovered
member.summary: Raised when the pointer enters/leaves a bar (leaving reports index -1).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode
member.summary: Renders a Code 128 (Set B) barcode as SVG rectangles. The module pattern comes from (pure, reflection-free); this component only draws it. Suitable for alphanumeric payloads (SKUs, tracking ids, URLs).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode.Value
member.summary: The text to encode (printable ASCII 32–126).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode.ModuleWidth
member.summary: Pixel width of a single module (narrowest bar).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode.BarHeight
member.summary: Height of the bars in pixels (excludes the caption).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode.ShowText
member.summary: Whether to print the encoded text beneath the bars.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBarcode.Class
member.summary: Extra CSS classes for the root svg.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxBoxPlot
member.summary: A box plot: one box-and-whisker per — Q1/median/Q3 box, whiskers to the nearest non-outlier min/max, and outlier dots beyond 1.5x IQR (Tukey's convention, ). Set to also draw a density silhouette behind each box, binned via the same compute backend as (Rust in the browser) over the shared value axis so every group's silhouette aligns. Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxBoxPlot.Violin
member.summary: Also draw a density silhouette (binned like ) behind each box.

member.summary: Separator glyph between items.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxBubbleChart
member.summary: A bubble chart: a scatter plot with a third dimension encoded as dot radius. Reuses the shared model — + place the bubble, and (when set) sizes it, linearly mapped across the series' own min/max into ... A point with no draws at . Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement — see 's remarks.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxBulletChart
member.summary: A bullet chart (Stephen Few's KPI design): one row per , each a measure bar against a target tick, optionally over qualitative range bands. Doesn't use — see for why. Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement (see 's remarks) — the interactive mark is a whole row.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxCalendar
member.summary: Tier 2 styled inline calendar built on : an always-visible month grid with previous/next/today navigation, single or range date selection (with a range hover preview), per-day marker dots, and an optional . The month is a real ARIA grid (row → gridcell) with 2-D arrow-key navigation, a focus-visible active day surfaced via aria-activedescendant, and a polite live region announcing month changes. Styling is token-driven (see dx-calendar.css).

member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement — see 's remarks; the interactive mark is the whole wick+body group for a candle.

member.summary: A slide carousel with previous/next controls, dot indicators, and Left/Right-arrow keyboard navigation. Slides wrap around. member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxChat
member.summary: An AI chat surface: a scrolling transcript of role-styled message bubbles plus a composer. Assistant messages render through (so the model's Markdown is shown safely), user messages render as plain text. A typing indicator shows while . Send with the button or Ctrl+Enter; receives the text and the composer clears. Styling is token-driven (see dx-chat.css).

Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-overlay.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxCommandPalette

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxContextMenu
member.summary: Tier 2 styled context menu. Wrap a region in it; right-clicking opens a menu at the cursor with keyboard navigation and dismissal. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-overlay.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxDataGrid`1

It inherits all behavior from and only supplies markup, built with and hardcoded sequence numbers so Blazor's linear-time diff stays intact across thousands of cells. member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxDocumentViewer
member.summary:
A multi-format document viewer. It dispatches on each document's : images render in an with zoom, PDFs in the browser's native viewer (no bundled PDF engine), Markdown through the safe renderer, and text/code in a 
. With more than one document a sidebar switches between them.
            A leaf component; styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-display.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxDocumentViewer.ShowToolbar
member.summary: Shows the action toolbar (title, kind badge, and — per kind — zoom, download, print, open-in-new-tab). On by default. The accessible download fallback link below an embedded PDF is shown regardless, so turning the toolbar off never strips the only way to obtain the file.

member.summary: Shows the Download control in the toolbar (where the kind supports it). On by default.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxDocumentViewer.ShowPrint
member.summary: Shows the Print control in the toolbar (PDF/image). On by default.

member.summary: Shows the Open-in-new-tab control in the toolbar (PDF/image). On by default.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxDocumentViewer.IsSafeSource(System.String)
member.summary: Decides whether a document is safe to place in an , , or . Allows relative URLs and a scheme allowlist (http, https, blob, plus data:image/* and data:application/pdf); rejects everything else — notably javascript:, vbscript:, file:, and HTML data URLs — to prevent script injection through the source value.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialAuthorBio
member.summary: A richer byline for a piece — avatar, name, role, and a short bio — composing the library's own rather than a plain text line. Typically placed once, near the end of a piece (the common "about the author" convention), leaving 's own hero byline as the compact top-of-page credit.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialAuthorBio.Initials
member.summary: Defaults to the first letter of the first and last words of .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialAuthorBio.ProfileUrl
member.summary: If set, the name links here (e.g. a profile page or the author's site).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialAuthorBio.ChildContent
member.summary: The bio text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialDissipation
member.summary: A "data-as-art" moment for a : a grid of dots that fade, shrink, and blur away once the parent stage gains its reveal class (see dx-editorial.css) — a CSS-only study, no canvas/WebGL.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialDropCap
member.summary: Wraps a paragraph — normally the opening paragraph of a piece — in an enlarged first-letter treatment, the oldest device in the magazine glossary (scribes used it to mark the start of a new section as early as the 15th century). Pure CSS (::first-letter) — the component's only job is applying the marker class to a real paragraph, since splitting the first character out in C# would break any inline markup (an <em>, a link) that happens to start the text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFigure
member.summary: A full-width, full-bleed narrative-break image for a piece. is the art itself — typically an , but any markup works (hand-authored SVG, etc.).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFigure.Caption
member.summary: Shown under the art. Keep it a plain factual caption, not restated body copy.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFooter
member.summary: A three-card footer grid for a piece — each card an eyebrow label, a title, a one-line body, and a link. Ships with BlazorDX-flavored defaults so a page always has something to show; pass to customize per piece.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFooter.FooterCard
member.summary: One footer card: an eyebrow label, a title, a one-line body, and where it links.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFooter.FooterCard.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: One footer card: an eyebrow label, a title, a one-line body, and where it links.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFooter.Cards
member.summary: Defaults to when omitted.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFootnoteRef
member.summary: An inline superscript marker jumping to the matching entry in a list — the web analogue of a print footnote/endnote.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialFootnotes
member.summary: The footnote list for a piece, typically placed at the end. Each entry gets a back-link to its matching marker.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialGlossaryTerm
member.summary: An inline term with a hover/focus definition for a piece — composes the library's own rather than a new interaction pattern. The visible term is a plain <span>, not naturally focusable, so it carries tabindex="0" itself to stay keyboard-reachable.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialInsetFigure
member.summary: A small, floated image with text wrapping around it via CSS shape-outside — a third image treatment for a piece, distinct from the full-bleed and the two-column . A classic print-magazine technique (text flowing around an inset image), well-supported natively in CSS since 2020. is the art itself — typically an , but hand-authored SVG works too, same convention as .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialInsetFigure.Right
member.summary: Floats right instead of left.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialLayout
member.summary: Shared "Architecture of Silence" editorial shell for long-form, magazine-style pieces: a hero (kicker, title, subtitle, byline, optionally a full-bleed photo) followed by a slot for the body, with a wrapped on automatically. Pure CSS (dx-editorial.css) — no Tailwind, no new color system; reuses the library's own dx-theme.css tokens throughout.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialLayout.HeroImageSrc
member.summary: Optional full-bleed image under the hero meta row. Omit for a text-only hero.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialPullQuote
member.summary: A large, italicized, serif-accented pull-quote to punctuate a long-form piece.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialReadingProgress
member.summary: A thin fixed bar across the top of the viewport that fills as the reader scrolls through a piece. Pure CSS via animation-timeline: scroll(root) — the scroll-driven-animations API, the same family as 's reveal — so there is no scroll-position listener and nothing runs per scroll frame. Because the fill is tied 1:1 to the reader's own scroll input rather than auto-playing, it's exempt from prefers-reduced-motion concerns (unlike the hero's Ken Burns zoom). Renders inert (0-width, invisible) in browsers without scroll-driven-animation support — never misleading, just absent. Purely decorative, so it's aria-hidden.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialRelated
member.summary: A "more like this" card row for the end of a piece. Plain non-generic entries (mirroring ) rather than a generic type parameter — map your own content model to with a simple Select before passing it in.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialScrollyStage
member.summary: One stage of a sequence — starts hidden/offset, then CSS-transitions in once the shared reveal script marks it visible.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialScrollyStage.Index
member.summary: A short label, e.g. "01" or "Stage 1". Also drives the ghost-numeral watermark via a data attribute.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialScrollyStage.Glyph
member.summary: A single decorative character/emoji shown in a small glyph box (aria-hidden).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialScrollyStage.Visual
member.summary: Optional custom visual (e.g. ) rendered below the body text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialScrollytelling
member.summary: A scroll-driven narrative section wrapping a sequence of children. The reveal is pure CSS transitions (dx-editorial.css's reveal class), toggled by an IntersectionObserver-only script — never a scroll-position listener, so nothing runs per scroll pixel. Add <script type="module" src="_content/BlazorDX.Components/dx-editorial-scrollytelling.js"></script> once to your app (alongside the dx-editorial.css link) to enable the reveal. Without it, stages render fully visible under the (scripting: none) CSS guard — but if scripting is enabled and the script tag is simply omitted, that guard won't apply, so stages stay hidden. The script is required, not optional, for the reveal to work.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSeriesNav
member.summary: Previous/next navigation for a multi-part piece — the web equivalent of a print magazine's jump line ("continued on p. 34"), except it moves forward into the next part rather than just resuming the current one. Either side may be omitted (the opening piece in a series has no previous; the closer has no next); when only one side is given, it's rendered alone rather than leaving an empty grid cell.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSidebar
member.summary: A floating technical-specification card for a piece — subtle background shift, doesn't interrupt the surrounding narrative's reading flow. Use for FSM states, crypto primitives, caveats, etc.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSidebar.Icon
member.summary: Optional leading glyph (a single emoji/character kept decorative via aria-hidden).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSidebar.Caveat
member.summary: An honest limitation/caveat, set off from the main spec body — for claims that need one.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSpread
member.summary: A two-column "classic meets modern" magazine spread for a piece: an elevated, drop-shadowed photo collaged against body copy, with a small labeled spec card overlapping its corner — the fashion-editorial "swatch card" device, adapted here to show a real fact (a cipher suite, a test name) instead of a color chip. Alternates media/text sides via for rhythm against the full-bleed breaks elsewhere in a piece. Stays on dx-theme.css tokens.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSpread.Kicker
member.summary: A short serif-italic label above the title — the "classic" counterpoint to the bold sans title.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSpread.SpecLabel
member.summary: Overlaps the photo's corner. Use a real fact, not decoration — e.g. a cipher suite or a test name.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialSpread.Reverse
member.summary: Puts the photo on the right instead of the left, for rhythm across a piece with more than one spread.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialStatRow
member.summary: A row of oversized numeric callouts for a piece — the data-journalism "big number" device. Use real facts, not decoration.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialTableOfContents
member.summary: A jump-link table of contents for a piece — the web descendant of a print magazine's contents page. Plain anchor links to caller-supplied target IDs; no scrollspy/active-section tracking in this version (a deliberate scope cut, not an oversight — most TOCs work fine without it).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialTableOfContents.TocEntry
member.summary: One entry: the link text and the id of the section it jumps to (without the leading '#').

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialTableOfContents.TocEntry.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: One entry: the link text and the id of the section it jumps to (without the leading '#').

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxEditorialTagList
member.summary: A row of topic/category pills for a piece — the web counterpart to a print magazine's "department" labeling. Each tag is a real link (not a , which has no href) since tags typically navigate to a filtered or archive view.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary
member.summary: Contains exceptions thrown while rendering its content so one broken component doesn't take down the page. On an error it reports to (resolved optionally — no-op if none is registered), raises , and shows a fallback: your ErrorContent template, or a styled default with a Retry button. Builds on the framework's .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary.OnError
member.summary: Raised with the caught exception (after it is reported to diagnostics).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary.FallbackTitle
member.summary: Heading shown by the default fallback.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary.ShowDetail
member.summary: Show the exception message in the default fallback (use only in development).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary.RetryText
member.summary: Label for the default fallback's recover button.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxErrorBoundary.Class
member.summary: Extra CSS classes appended to the default fallback.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager
member.summary:
Tier 2 styled file manager built on : a breadcrumb, a folder navigation tree on the left, and the selected folder's contents on the right. Double-click a folder to drill in, or a file to open it.
Drag-and-drop is a hybrid enhancement layered on top, never the only path: items can be dragged to move them within or between panes, and OS files can be dropped onto the contents pane to upload — both via a native HTML5 DnD bridge (). The always-available equivalents are a keyboard/single-pointer "mark then place" move (WCAG 2.5.7) and a standard upload control. Move/upload outcomes are announced through an aria-live region (4.1.3). Styling is token-driven (see dx-filemanager.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.VerifyIntegrity
member.summary: When true, each uploaded file is hashed in the browser (Web Crypto) and re-hashed here from the received stream; the two hashes are compared and the per-file outcome is raised through so corruption in transit is caught before the host writes anything. Off by default and a no-op off-browser (the client hash is unavailable), where files simply upload unverified.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.HashAlgorithm
member.summary: Hash algorithm for . Defaults to SHA-256.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.MaxVerifySize
member.summary: Max bytes read per file when re-hashing an upload (the read-stream cap). Default 10 MB.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.OnUploadVerified
member.summary: Raised after an upload when is on, with each file's result.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.OnFilesDropped
member.summary: Raised when OS files are dropped on the contents pane (native DnD). Carries the dropped files' metadata and the destination folder (null = root). Streaming the bytes is the host's job; the always-available path uses with real streams instead.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxFileManager.OnUpload
member.summary: Raised when files are chosen through the always-available upload control.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.FileDropArgs
member.summary: OS files dropped on the file manager via native drag-and-drop, plus their destination.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.FileDropArgs.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Interop.DroppedFile},BlazorDX.Primitives.Files.FileSystemEntry)
member.summary: OS files dropped on the file manager via native drag-and-drop, plus their destination.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.FileIntegrityResult
member.summary: The per-file outcome of an upload integrity check (browser hash vs. re-hash on receipt).
member.param: The uploaded file's name.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxGraph
member.summary: A single dynamic entry point over the 18 chart kinds whose data reduces to one of three already-shared, strongly-typed shapes: (13 kinds), a root (Treemap/Sunburst), or a bare scalar/raw-sample list (the gauges, Histogram). Switching at runtime — e.g. a dropdown toggling the same series between Bar/Line/Area — re-renders through the matching underlying component; no new markup, no re-binding .

remarks.para: This is a facade, not a rewrite: every case below opens the real Dx*Chart component via and forwards typed parameters — zero reflection, zero boxing, and the compiler still catches a typo'd parameter name at the call site (inside this file) exactly as it would in hand-written markup.

Why only these 18, not all 25: the other 7 chart types (DxBulletChart, DxBoxPlot, DxSankeyChart, DxNetworkGraph, DxParallelCoordinates, DxWordCloud, DxChordDiagram) each need their own dedicated data record (BulletPoint, BoxPlotGroup, SankeyNode/SankeyLink, etc.) that no other kind reuses. Adding one of those kinds to would cost exactly one new parameter (or pair) for exactly one kind — no consolidation benefit, just a wider surface on the shared facade. The 18 kinds here were chosen because they're "free riders": 13 kinds share , 2 share , and the gauges/Histogram need only BCL primitives (, of ) — no new record type at all. Those 7 stay as their own named components, used directly — which is exactly what a fundamentally different data shape calls for.

member.summary: Used by .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxHeatmap
member.summary: A heatmap: a grid of colored cells, one per (row, column) pair. Reuses the shared model — is the row key, is the column key, and is the cell's value. Intensity is drawn as fill-opacity on the chart's accent color (normalized min→max across every cell), not a hand-rolled color scale — theme-safe, and colour is never the only signal (the value is always in the cell's tooltip/label). Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement — see 's remarks; the interactive mark is one grid cell, in Points order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxHeatmap.ShowValues
member.summary: Show each cell's value as text (in addition to the tooltip).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxHistogram
member.summary: A histogram: bins a raw value series into equal-width buckets and draws the per-bin counts as bars. The binning runs on the compute backend (Rust in the browser, managed C# on the server), so large datasets bin off the UI thread. Styling is token-driven (see dx-chart.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxImageEditor
member.summary: A canvas-backed image editor: load an image, adjust brightness / contrast / saturation, toggle grayscale / sepia / invert, rotate and flip, then download the result. Editing is non-destructive — every change repaints the canvas from the original decoded bitmap (held in the TypeScript bridge). The .NET side owns the edit state; the DOM/canvas work happens in image-editor.js via . Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-display.css).

member.summary: A text input that formats its value against a fixed mask as the user types. Mask tokens: 0 = digit, A = letter, * = letter or digit; any other character is a literal that is inserted automatically. A leaf component; the masking is pure and reflection-free (see ).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxMaskedTextBox.ApplyMask(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Formats input against . Significant characters are placed into the token positions; literals are inserted as needed and any literals the user typed are skipped. Trailing literals (with no further input) are omitted. Reflection-free and pure — safe to unit test directly.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxProgress
member.summary: A progress bar. Determinate when is set (0–100); indeterminate (animated) when null. WAI-ARIA progressbar. Styling via dx-layout.css.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxQrCode
member.summary: Renders a QR code as SVG. The module matrix comes from (pure, reflection-free; versions 1–4, all EC levels); this component only draws it, with a standard quiet zone. Colours use CSS variables, though scanners need dark-on-light contrast.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxQrCode.Value
member.summary: The payload to encode.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxQrCode.Level
member.summary: Error-correction level (higher tolerates more damage but holds less data).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxQrCode.ModuleSize
member.summary: Pixel size of a single module.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxQrCode.Class
member.summary: Extra CSS classes for the root svg.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxQueryBuilder
member.summary: A visual query builder: nestable AND/OR groups of field-operator-value conditions over a declared field list. Edits the bound tree in place; evaluate it with QueryEvaluator. Styling is token-driven (see dx-querybuilder.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxQueryBuilder.OnChange
member.summary: Raised whenever the query tree changes.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadarChart
member.summary: A radar (spider) chart: one polygon per series over a shared set of . Reuses the shared model: each point's matches one of and its groups it into a named series. Pure SVG, no JS or DI; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement (see 's remarks). A small vertex dot is drawn at each (series, axis) intersection as the interactive mark once and/or are wired. The legend is always click/keyboard-operable — clicking a series name hides its polygon and vertices and raises .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadarChart.Axes
member.summary: Axis labels (one per spoke); points align to these by .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadarChart.Points
member.summary: The points to plot; grouped by , placed by .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadarChart.Max
member.summary: Axis maximum; 0 (default) auto-scales to the largest value.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadarChart.Rings
member.summary: Number of concentric grid rings.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadialGauge
member.summary: A radial (circular) gauge: a 270° arc track with a value arc and a center readout. Reports its value as an ARIA meter. Pure SVG. Styling is token-driven (see dx-chart.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadialGauge.Color

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadialGauge.Format
member.summary: Display format for the center value (e.g. "0", "0.0", "P0").

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRadioGroup`1
member.summary: A single-select radio group following the WAI-ARIA radio pattern: one tab stop, arrow keys move and select (roving tabindex). Reuses the roving-tabindex primitive. Two-way bind via @bind-Value. Styling is CSS-variable driven.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRangeSlider

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRating
member.summary: A star rating input. Click or use the keyboard (Arrow keys, Home/End) to set a value; hovering previews. Follows the WAI-ARIA slider pattern. A leaf component: the behavior is small enough that no separate primitive is warranted. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-layout.css).

member.summary: A color toolbar command: foreColor (text) or hiliteColor (highlight) paired with a CSS color. Passed to a model-driven host via .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ValueCommandArgs
member.summary: A value-carrying toolbar command: fontName (family) or fontSize (points) paired with the chosen value. Passed to a model-driven host via .

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.ValueCommandArgs.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A value-carrying toolbar command: fontName (family) or fontSize (points) paired with the chosen value. Passed to a model-driven host via .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor
member.summary: A WYSIWYG rich-text editor over a contentEditable region with a formatting toolbar. Edited HTML is read back from the DOM and routed through an injected before it becomes — so the application's own vetted policy decides what HTML survives (BlazorDX ships no HTML parser of its own). Without a real sanitizer the default policy renders content inert; supply one for actual rich editing. Two-way bind via @bind-Value. Styling is token-driven (see dx-richtext.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.Sanitizer
member.summary: The sanitizer applied to edited HTML. Defaults to an inert (encode-all) policy.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.OnCommand
member.summary: Optional interceptor for the model-drivable toolbar commands: bold, italic, underline, strikeThrough, removeFormat, and the four justify* alignments. When set, those buttons invoke this callback instead of document.execCommand, so a host can apply the edit to its own model and re-seed the surface (the model-driven editing core, ADR-0015). All other tools keep their built-in behavior.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.OnUndo
member.summary: Invoked for the undo keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd+Z), so a host can drive its own history instead of the browser's contentEditable undo.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.OnRedo
member.summary: Invoked for the redo keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd+Y or Shift+Ctrl/Cmd+Z).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.OnColorCommand
member.summary: Optional interceptor for the color inputs (foreColor text, hiliteColor highlight). When set, picking a color invokes this callback instead of applying the color through the browser, so a model-driven host owns the edit (ADR-0015).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.OnValueCommand
member.summary: Optional interceptor for the value dropdowns (fontName family, fontSize points). When set, choosing a value invokes this instead of touching the DOM, so a model-driven host owns the edit (ADR-0015).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.PromptLinkAsync
member.summary: Prompts for a URL (http/https/mailto only) and returns it, or empty on cancel — the model-driven host then sets the link itself.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.PickImageAsync
member.summary: Opens an image file picker and returns "mimeType|base64", or empty on cancel — the host turns it into a model image block.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.GetSelectionRangeAsync
member.summary: The current owned selection as "containerIndex,start,end" (see ), or empty if unaddressable.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.SetSelectionRangeAsync(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Restores a selection by run-container index and character offsets, refocusing the editor — used after a model edit re-renders the surface.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.ReseedAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Replaces the editing surface's HTML in place (no re-mount), so a model-driven host can push a freshly rendered model into the DOM and then restore the caret with . The HTML is sanitized like the initial seed.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.FindNextAsync(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Selects and scrolls to the next (or previous) occurrence of in the editing surface, wrapping at the ends. Returns the 1-based match index, or 0 if there are no matches. The selection is owned via the bridge (no model mapping).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.DxRichTextEditor.GetTableCellAsync
member.summary: Reports the caret's table position as "tableIndex,rowIndex,colIndex" (0-based, table index in document order), or an empty string when the caret is not in a table.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxSankeyChart
member.summary: A Sankey diagram: nodes as columns of rectangles, links as thickness-scaled ribbons flowing between them. Layered layout via — see its remarks for what "layered" does and doesn't do (not full crossing-minimization). Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Nodes are independently focusable (natural tab order) when is wired — see 's remarks for why this differs from the roving-index pattern the flat charts use. Links are not separately selectable, only hoverable via a title tooltip — a ribbon has no single natural keyboard target the way a node rect does.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxScatterChart
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement — see 's remarks.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxScheduler
member.summary: Tier 2 styled scheduler built on . Renders a toolbar with a keyboard-accessible Week / Month / Day view switch, then the active view: a time grid (Week/Day) with absolutely positioned event blocks, or a month date grid with per-day event buttons. All views share one ARIA "grid" with 2-D arrow-key navigation (mirroring the DataGrid), focus-visible cells, accessible event buttons, text category labels (never colour alone), and a polite aria-live region announcing view/date changes. Styling is token-driven (see dx-scheduler.css).
member.remarks: RRULE-style recurring events are expanded in C# (see ), and the time grid supports pointer drag-to-move / drag-to-create via a thin TypeScript bridge () with edge auto-scroll — all date math and state stay in C#. The overlap layout is computed in pure C# (the once-planned Rust overlap-lane kernel is not wired up and the date math does not need it).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxScheduler.HourHeight
member.summary: Pixel height of one hour row (Week/Day time grid).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxSelect`1
member.summary: Tier 2 styled single-select dropdown. Inherits all behavior from and renders a trigger plus an anchored, keyboard-navigable option list with WAI-ARIA listbox semantics. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-overlay.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxSheet
member.summary: A sheet / offcanvas panel that slides in from a screen edge. It is a (same focus-trap, scroll-lock, and dismissal) with edge-anchored positioning and a slide transition instead of a centered panel. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-overlay.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxSheet.Side
member.summary: Edge the sheet slides from: left, right, top, or bottom.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxSkeleton
member.summary: A shimmering placeholder block shown while content loads. Decorative (aria-hidden). Styling via dx-layout.css.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxSkipLink
member.summary:
A "skip to main content" link for WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks). Render it as the very first focusable element in your layout: it stays visually offscreen until focused, then appears, letting keyboard and screen-reader users jump past repeated navigation straight to the main region.
Give your main content element a matching id (and tabindex="-1" so it can take focus): <main id="main-content" tabindex="-1">. Styling is token-driven (see dx-layout.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxSkipLink.TargetId
member.summary: Fragment id of the main-content target (default main-content).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxSkipLink.Text
member.summary: Link text (default "Skip to main content").

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxSkipLink.Class
member.summary: Extra CSS classes appended to the link.

Resizing uses the same pure-Blazor pointer-overlay technique as the data grid's column resize (a full-window overlay captures pointer move/up so the gesture never loses the thin divider). Arrow keys nudge the divider for keyboard users. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-display.css).

member.summary: A multi-series bar chart, stacked by default or grouped side-by-side when is false. Categories run along the x axis; a legend names the series. member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxTransferList
member.summary: A dual list box: move items between an "available" and a "selected" list. It is a pure composition of two (multi-select) plus move buttons — no new behavior, demonstrating how components assemble from the shared engine. Value is the set of items currently in the selected list.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxTreeGrid`1
member.summary: Tier 2 styled tree grid: a hierarchical table built on . The first column carries the expand/ collapse twisty and indentation; the rest render like a normal grid. Markup uses with static sequence numbers and the inherited virtualization. Styling is CSS-variable-based (see dx-datagrid.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxTreemap
member.summary: A treemap: nested rectangles sized proportionally to , laid out with the squarified algorithm () so cells stay close to square instead of degenerating into slivers. Only leaves are drawn; a leaf's color comes from its nearest ancestor with a override, or a palette color assigned per top-level branch. Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is opt-in like the rest of the chart family, but — unlike the flat charts' roving — each cell is independently focusable (natural tab order), since a nested hierarchy doesn't reduce to one linear index the way a bar or slice list does.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ChartTreeNodeEventArgs
member.summary: The node a click/Enter/Space interaction occurred on, for /.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxTreeView
member.summary: A hierarchical tree view with expand/collapse, single selection, and keyboard navigation following the WAI-ARIA tree pattern: one tab stop, a roving active node tracked with aria-activedescendant, Arrow keys to move/expand/collapse, and Enter/Space to select. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-display.css).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualize`1
member.summary: Renders only the items intersecting the scroll viewport (plus a small overscan), keeping the DOM small for arbitrarily large lists. This is the grid's virtualization windowing, generalized into a reusable component for any fixed-height list (option lists, logs, timelines). Reuses the shared scroll/DOM bridge; off-browser it renders the initial window.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualize`1.ItemHeight
member.summary: Fixed height of each row in pixels (the windowing math depends on it).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualize`1.Role
member.summary: Optional ARIA role for the scroll container (e.g. "rowgroup" when the virtualizer hosts the body rows of a role="grid"). Defaults to none.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualize`1.ItemRole
member.summary: Optional ARIA role for each item wrapper. Set to "presentation" so the wrapper does not interrupt a grid/row/cell hierarchy supplied by the child content; defaults to none (a plain element).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualize`1.TabIndex
member.summary: Optional tabindex for the scroll container. Because the container is the scrollable region, set this to 0 so it is keyboard-reachable when the items themselves are not individually focusable (WCAG 2.1.1).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualKeyboard
member.summary: An on-screen QWERTY keyboard for touch, kiosk, and accessibility text entry. Keys edit a two-way-bound ; Shift toggles letter case and the number row's symbols, Backspace deletes, Space inserts a space, and Enter raises (it does not insert a newline). A leaf component; styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-display.css).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualKeyboard.Value
member.summary: The text being edited. Two-way bindable via @bind-Value.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualKeyboard.ValueChanged
member.summary: Raised whenever changes from a key press.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualKeyboard.OnEnter
member.summary: Raised when the Enter key is pressed (e.g. to submit).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxVirtualKeyboard.Class
member.summary: Extra CSS classes appended to the keyboard container.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxWaterfallChart
member.summary: A waterfall chart: bars float from a running total to show how a sequence of positive/negative deltas add up to a final value. Reuses the shared model — a point's is a delta added to the running total (positive or negative), unless is set, in which case the point is an absolute "total" bar (drawn from zero) that resets the running total to . Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.
member.remarks: Selection is a progressive enhancement — see 's remarks.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxWaterfallChart.UpColor
member.summary: Color for a positive delta bar.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxWaterfallChart.DownColor
member.summary: Color for a negative delta bar.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.DxWaterfallChart.TotalColor
member.summary: Color for an absolute "total" bar (a point with set).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.DxWordCloud
member.summary: A word cloud: words sized by , spiral-packed () so they fill the available space without overlapping. A word that can't find room within the chart's bounds is silently dropped by the layout — no truncation error, it just doesn't appear (a caller with a `Words.Count` vs. rendered-count mismatch can detect this). Pure SVG; styling via dx-chart.css.

member.summary: A broker's response to an ephemeral chat session handshake: its ephemeral P-256 public key and an AES-GCM payload encrypted for the shared secret that key agrees to with the client's public key from . Never carries key material -- only what needs to hand to CompleteAndMountAsync.
member.param: The broker's ephemeral P-256 public key (uncompressed SEC1, base64).

member.summary: What a field template receives: the field metadata, its current value, a callback to change it, and any validation errors against it.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.FormContext
member.summary: Cascaded by so child components (, containers) can render fields without knowing the model's type. It exposes string get/set over the generated descriptor plus any author-supplied templates.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.FormContext.IdPrefix
member.summary: Unique-per-form id stem, used to build stable element ids (e.g. error regions for aria-describedby). Set by .

member.name: E:BlazorDX.Components.FormContext.Changed
member.summary: Raised when any field value or the validation state changes, so manually laid-out s re-render even when the model is mutated elsewhere (e.g. an AI tool call).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.FormFieldRenderer
member.summary: Renders a single field — label, the input appropriate to its , and validation errors — honoring any // . Shared by the auto-render loop and .

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.MarkdownRenderer
member.summary: Converts a safe subset of Markdown to HTML. Security is structural: every text run is HTML-encoded before any formatting tags are inserted, so the only tags in the output are the fixed structural set this renderer emits — user input can never introduce a tag. Link targets are checked against a scheme allow-list (so javascript: URLs are dropped). This is the one audited place outside BlazorDX.Security that builds a from runtime data; correctness here is the safety guarantee.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.MarkdownRenderer.Render(System.String)
member.summary: Renders Markdown to a sanitized ready to display.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Components.ToastService
member.summary:
Queues transient toast notifications and auto-dismisses them. Registered with a scoped lifetime (never Singleton — that would share notifications across users on Blazor Server, per the BlazorDX state-isolation rule). A DxToastHost subscribes to to render the active toasts.
Auto-dismiss can be paused () and resumed () so a hovered or keyboard-focused toast is not whisked away mid-read — the host wires these to pointer/focus events for WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable).

member.name: E:BlazorDX.Components.ToastService.OnChange
member.summary: Raised whenever the set of active toasts changes.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.ToastService.Show(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Shows a toast and removes it automatically after . A non-positive duration makes the toast sticky (dismissed only by the user).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.ToastService.PauseAll
member.summary: Pauses every auto-dismiss countdown (e.g. while the toast region is hovered or focused), so a timed notification cannot disappear before it is read.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Components.ToastService.ResumeAll
member.summary: Restarts auto-dismiss for every visible toast (e.g. when the pointer or focus leaves the region). Each toast gets a fresh countdown.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.SessionId
member.summary: Opaque session identifier from the host. No auth logic happens here.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.ServerPublicKeyBase64
member.summary: The server's ephemeral P-256 public key (uncompressed SEC1, base64), for the ECDH handshake. Required unless is , in which case it is ignored -- supplies it instead.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.NonceBase64
member.summary: The AES-GCM nonce (base64) for . Required unless is .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.CiphertextBase64
member.summary: The AES-GCM ciphertext, including its trailing authentication tag (base64). Required unless is .

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.UseLiveHandshake
member.summary: When , this component drives the full handshake itself instead of expecting an already-encrypted payload: it calls to generate a fresh client keypair in-browser, hands the resulting public key to , and mounts whatever that returns. , , and are ignored in this mode. This is the only mode in which a real deployment can work: the ciphertext must be encrypted for a public key that does not exist until the client generates it, so it can never be a value known ahead of a page render (see docs/adr/0016).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.EstablishSession
member.summary: Required when is : an async callback that takes the client's freshly generated public key (uncompressed SEC1, base64) and returns the broker's encrypted response. This component never talks to a broker directly -- the host supplies this delegate (an HTTP call to BlazorDX.Conduit's conduit in a real deployment, or any in-process mock for a demo) so the component stays broker-agnostic.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.EventsBaseUrl
member.summary: Base path for the revocation SSE endpoint; the bridge connects to {EventsBaseUrl}/{SessionId}. Defaults to the contract path implemented by EphemeralEventsEndpoint.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.OnWithdrawn
member.summary: Raised after the server pushes a WITHDRAW event and the node has been scrubbed.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.OnRefreshed
member.summary: Raised after the server pushes a REFRESH event (the node is left mounted).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Components.SecureEphemeralChat.OnTamperDetected
member.summary: Raised if the MutationObserver detects tampering and the node self-destructs.
